name: Embedded Firmware Reverse Engineering and Device Trust Abuse
description: Attack tree for extracting firmware, reversing device trust boundaries, and turning embedded weaknesses into code execution, hidden control, or persistent access.
focus_areas: Boot chain, update path, and trust-anchor analysis
Protocol abuse and device persistence planning
prompt_hints: Prefer binwalk, UART, JTAG, QEMU, filesystem recovery, bootloader trust, and protocol-reversing workflows over generic IoT compromise language.
Model the device's real trust boundaries between boot ROM, bootloader, firmware image, management services, and OTA or local update paths.
root_objective: Reverse engineer embedded firmware to identify exploitable trust boundaries and gain persistent control of the device

nodes.title: Gain Persistent Control Through Firmware Reversing and Trust Abuse
nodes.description: Acquire the firmware image, recover the device's trust model, and turn embedded weaknesses into code execution, privileged control, or durable persistence.
nodes.attack_surface: Firmware image, management services, OTA path, physical debug interfaces
nodes.required_access: Device sample, OTA package, or field access
nodes.required_tools: binwalk, Ghidra, UART/JTAG, QEMU, firmware emulation tools

nodes.title: Acquire Firmware and Recover Runtime Layout
nodes.description: Obtain a representative image and reconstruct the filesystem, services, and startup flow that define the device's reachable attack surface.
nodes.attack_surface: OTA package, flash dump, serial console, vendor support bundle

nodes.title: Extract Firmware via OTA Package, Flash Dump, UART, or JTAG
nodes.description: Collect the real firmware image from update bundles or direct hardware access so analysis is based on production code rather than vendor documentation alone.
nodes.attack_surface: SPI flash, eMMC, OTA package, maintenance port

nodes.title: Unpack Filesystem and Recover Services, Credentials, and Startup Logic
nodes.description: Rebuild the root filesystem, init scripts, service configs, and web or RPC handlers to understand how the device boots and which trust anchors it relies on.
nodes.attack_surface: Init scripts, service configs, local secrets, CGI handlers

nodes.title: Reverse Boot Chain and Update Trust Model
nodes.description: Determine where signature checks, rollback protections, and trust anchors exist across bootloader, kernel, rootfs, and update packages.
nodes.attack_surface: Secure boot, recovery mode, OTA package validation
nodes.required_tools: Ghidra, U-Boot tooling, diffing, serial console

nodes.title: Identify Bootloader, Recovery, and Signature Validation Gaps
nodes.description: Reverse bootloader and recovery logic to determine whether unsigned images, weak verification, or developer backdoors permit untrusted code to boot.

nodes.title: Trace OTA Package Validation and Rollback Policy
nodes.description: Recover how the device authenticates OTA manifests and image metadata, including downgrade acceptance, version checks, and key rotation behavior.
nodes.attack_surface: Manifest parser, image headers, OTA transport

nodes.title: Recover Reachable Management Surfaces and Secrets
nodes.description: Use the recovered firmware to identify the services, protocols, and credentials that create practical entry points or privilege boundaries on the live device.
nodes.attack_surface: Web UI, SSH/Telnet, proprietary RPC, MQTT, local maintenance interfaces

nodes.title: Recover Hardcoded Credentials, Keys, or Hidden Maintenance Endpoints
nodes.description: Mine configuration files, binaries, and scripts for static credentials, service tokens, debug accounts, or vendor maintenance verbs that remain enabled in production.
nodes.attack_surface: Config files, binaries, startup scripts, hidden endpoints

nodes.title: Reverse Proprietary Protocol Handler or Command Parser
nodes.description: Recover the parser state machine and authentication assumptions of the device's network or local management protocol to find malformed-command or trust-boundary abuse paths.
nodes.attack_surface: TCP, UDP, serial, BLE, or local RPC handler

nodes.title: Turn Firmware Findings into Persistent Device Control
nodes.description: Translate the reversed trust model and recovered weaknesses into an implant, malicious update, or privileged control path that survives reboots or service restarts.
nodes.attack_surface: Boot chain, OTA path, startup services, writable partitions
nodes.required_tools: Custom firmware image, update tooling, debugger

nodes.title: Deploy Malicious Firmware, Plugin, or Startup Modification Matching Device Trust
nodes.description: Package the exploit or implant in the exact form the device trusts, whether that means OTA image, plugin bundle, writable init script, or bootloader environment change.
nodes.attack_surface: OTA update path, writable filesystem, startup environment
nodes.required_privileges: User, technician, or trusted update path
nodes.required_tools: Firmware repacker, signing analysis, upload tooling
